'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Fukui Prefecture|Q133879}}{{jalink|Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541}}{{jalink|Wakasa|Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（{{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964|myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建=(trad.) {{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1 ({{jalink|701|Q23496}}) |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|April 8|Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{jalink|Wakasa|Q1349039}}, {{jalink|Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541}}, {{jalink|Fukui Prefecture|Q133879}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{jalink|Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{jalink|701|Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{jalink|Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|Hiruga}}, present {{jalink|Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{jalink|Hyūga Province|Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}, crossed the {{jalink|Sea of Japan|Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{jalink|Hokurikudō|Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|monthly}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{jalink|Kenmu|Q1193970}} and {{jalink|Genki|Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{jalink|Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{jalink|chōzubachi|Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{jalink|1851|Q7626}} ({{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Fukui Prefecture|Q133879}}{{jalink|Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541}}{{jalink|Wakasa|Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（{{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964|myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建=(trad.) {{jalink|Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|Taihō}} 1 ({{jalink|701|Q23496}}) |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |例祭={{jalink|April 8|Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{jalink|Wakasa|Q1349039}}, {{jalink|Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541}}, {{jalink|Fukui Prefecture|Q133879}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{jalink|Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{jalink|701|Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{jalink|Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|Hiruga}}, present {{jalink|Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{jalink|Hyūga Province|Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{jalink|Izumo Province|Q582846}}, crossed the {{jalink|Sea of Japan|Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{jalink|myōjin taisha|Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{jalink|Hokurikudō|Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{jalink|Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|monthly}} and {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{jalink|Kenmu|Q1193970}} and {{jalink|Genki|Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{jalink|Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{jalink|chōzubachi|Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{jalink|1851|Q7626}} ({{jalink|Kaei|Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 260630186 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1210005006318 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01052101 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福井県三方上中郡若狭町気山字寺谷129-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 260630186 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1210005006318 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01052101 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福井県三方上中郡若狭町気山字寺谷129-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630|1=Wakasahiko Shrine}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} and {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga|1=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384|1=Mihama, Fukui}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737|1=Hyūga Province}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239|1=Hokurikudō}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|1=Kenmu}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094|1=Namekata Kyūbei}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160|1=Chōzubachi}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626|1=Kuantan Singingi Regency}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 260630186 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1210005006318 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01052101 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福井県三方上中郡若狭町気山字寺谷129-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630|1=Wakasahiko Shrine}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} and {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga|1=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384|1=Mihama, Fukui}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737|1=Hyūga Province}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239|1=Hokurikudō}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|1=Kenmu}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094|1=Namekata Kyūbei}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160|1=Chōzubachi}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626|1=Kuantan Singingi Regency}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'福井県三方上中郡若狭町気山字寺谷129-5', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波西神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449531}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Uwase Shrine |画像=[[File:Uwase jinja.JPG|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} Kiyama, Aza Teradani 129-5 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Taihō (Japan)|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Uwase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Uwase Shrine''' (うわせじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at 129-5 Teradani, Kiyama, {{ill|lt=Wakasa|Q1349039|WD=Q1349039|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Mikata-Kaminaka District|Q1204541|WD=Q1204541|1=Mikatakaminaka District, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Hikonagisatake Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} (うがやふきあわせずのみこと) At nearby {{ill|lt=Wakasahiko Shrine|Q11616630|WD=Q11616630|1=Wakasahiko Shrine}}, his parent deities {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}} and {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} are enshrined. === History === === Tradition === According to tradition, it was founded on March 8, {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} (Taihō 1) at {{ill|lt=Hiruga, Mihama (Fukui)|Q113625481|WD=Q113625481|lt=Hiruga|1=Hiruga}}, present {{ill|lt=Mihama (Fukui)|Q629384|WD=Q629384|1=Mihama, Fukui}}, then moved to the foot of Kanemukai-yama in Uenodani, and moved again to the current site in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1). In Hiruga tradition, a fisherman named Rokurōemon of the village was fishing on the lake when a cormorant appeared and told him that a noble person was at the bottom of the lake seeking rescue. Led by the bird into the lake, he found a treasured sword on the lakebed, brought it home, and enshrined it on his household altar. Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto then appeared in his dream and told him to enshrine him by the upper lakeshore and to name the village “Hyūga,” the name of his own land. The shrine name Uwase derives from “Uwase” (upper shoal). Another tradition says that in the Taihō era, {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} came from {{ill|lt=Hyūga Province|Q513737|WD=Q513737|1=Hyūga Province}} in Kyushu, passed through {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}, crossed the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|WD=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}}, and appeared here. The Hiruga area is thought to have had migration from Hyūga in ancient times, and many dialect forms and place names are shared with Hyūga. === Medieval / early modern === In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} and is the only one among 352 shrines on the {{ill|lt=Hokurikudō|Q3139239|WD=Q3139239|1=Hokurikudō}} route to receive offerings for {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|lt=monthly|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. The shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare twice, in the {{ill|lt=Kenmu|Q1193970|WD=Q1193970|1=Kenmu}} and {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} eras. === Modern / contemporary === When {{ill|lt=Namekata Kyūbei|Q128852094|WD=Q128852094|1=Namekata Kyūbei}} dug the Urami River connecting Lake Suigetsu and Lake Kugushi in the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} era (1661-1673), he prayed at Uwase Shrine and the difficult engineering work succeeded. He arranged donation of two tan of rice fields as shrine land and personally dedicated a {{ill|lt=chōzubachi|Q2972160|WD=Q2972160|1=Chōzubachi}} stone basin{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=100 Notable Figures of Fukui Hometown |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |date=2017 |page=65 |isbn=}}. This basin is still placed in the precincts"[https://www.bousai.go.jp/kyoiku/kyokun/kyoukunnokeishou/rep/1662_kanbun_omiwakasa_jishin/pdf/1662-kanbun-omiwakasaJISHIN_08_chap4.pdf Chapter 4: Damage, land change, and disaster response in Wakasa Province]" in ''Report (1662 Kanbun Ōmi/Wakasa Earthquake)'', Cabinet Office. In {{ill|lt=1851|Q7626|WD=Q7626|1=Kuantan Singingi Regency}} ({{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 4), the present shrine buildings were constructed. On April 24, 2015, it was recognized as part of the Japan Heritage "Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital - Miketsukuni Wakasa and Saba Kaidō"{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story005/ |title=Wakasa exchange cultural heritage linking sea and capital |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-19 }}. === Festivals === * April 8 (one-month-late observance for lunar March 8) - Annual festival with dedication of dengaku, Ō-no-mai, lion dance, and Urayasu dance{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/159558 |title=Ritual performing arts of Uwase Shrine |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2015-11-29}}. === Cultural properties === === National selected intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Fukui Prefecture intangible folk cultural property === * "Uwase Shrine Festival" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20160118_113852 Uwase Shrine] Fukui Prefecture Shrine Office {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわせしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwase Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Wakasa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福井県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:若狭国の式内社|名うわせしんしや]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:若狭町の歴史]] [[Category:若狭町の建築物]] [[Category:日本遺産]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
